perturbation.original_text: This letter agreement shall terminate on the date that is two years following the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This letter agreement shall terminate on the date that is one year following the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that the agreement terminates two years following the initial date. The modification changed it to one year after the initial date. This introduces an in-text contradiction directly because the original text states a period that conflicts with the new changed duration. The term of agreement is now ambiguous, creating a conflict and impacting enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This letter agreement shall terminate on the date that is one year following the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement terminates two years after the initial date. The changed text modifies this to one year. The contradicted text states the agreement terminates one year after the initial date. While the changed text and contradicted text are now the same, the original text stated a different period. Thus, there is a contradiction between the original and changed texts regarding the agreement's term. The change directly causes a conflict with the original meaning of the term, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the provided perturbation type Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement, and (C) be advised of the terms of this letter agreement and directed to comply with the terms hereof to the same extent as if they were parties to this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing the obligation for representatives to be advised of the terms of the letter agreement and directed to comply with them as if they were parties to the agreement. This contradicts the original intent of ensuring representatives are bound by the same obligations as the parties, now making it uncertain how strictly the confidentiality obligations should be enforced upon the representatives. The uncertainty affects enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All such Representatives shall (A) be informed by you of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and (B) agree to treat the Evaluation Material in accordance with this letter agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that representatives be advised of and comply with the terms of the letter agreement as if they were parties to it. This directly contradicts the original intent of ensuring representatives are bound by the same obligations, creating uncertainty about the enforcement of confidentiality obligations. Even though the Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, the Explanation makes it clear that removing the term creates an in-text contradiction regarding the representatives' obligations to the confidentiality agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company’s demand, you shall, at your sole option, either promptly (a) destroy the Evaluation Material and any copies thereof, or (b) return to the Company all Evaluation Material and any copies thereof, and, in either case, confirm in writing to the Company that all such material has been destroyed or returned, as applicable, in compliance with this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company’s demand, you shall promptly (a) destroy the Evaluation Material and any copies thereof, or (b) return to the Company all Evaluation Material and any copies thereof, and, in either case, confirm in writing to the Company that all such material has been destroyed or returned, as applicable, in compliance with this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the clause, stating that choosing a path for destroying Evaluation Material is at the sole option of the party that is being asked to destroy them. Now with the change made, it's up to the Company on which method should be taken to destroying Evaluation Material. This creates an in-text contradiction, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company’s demand, you shall promptly (a) destroy the Evaluation Material and any copies thereof, or (b) return to the Company all Evaluation Material and any copies thereof, and, in either case, confirm in writing to the Company that all such material has been destroyed or returned, as applicable, in compliance with this letter agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the party receiving the demand (you) has the option to choose between destroying or returning the Evaluation Material. The changed text removes this option for the receiving party. Because the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, the changed text does in fact contradict what the original text was stating; it is now up to the Company to make the determination instead of the party receiving the demand. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it alters a key decision-making power outlined in the original contract, directly impacting enforcement by creating uncertainty about who decides the method of destruction/return. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies.
